NKJV.1: Do we begin again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, as some others, epistles of commendation to you or letters of commendation from you?
NKJV.2: You are our epistle written in our hearts, known and read by all men;
NKJV.3: clearly you are an epistle of Christ, ministered by us, written not with ink but by the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of flesh, that is, of the heart.
NKJV.4: And we have such trust through Christ toward God.
NKJV.5: Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think of anything as being from ourselves, but our sufficiency is from God,
NKJV.6: who also made us sufficient as ministers of the new covenant, not of the letter but of the Spirit; for the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
NKJV.7: But if the ministry of death, written and engraved on stones, was glorious, so that the children of Israel could not look steadily at the face of Moses because of the glory of his countenance, which glory was passing away,
NKJV.8: how will the ministry of the Spirit not be more glorious?
NKJV.9: For if the ministry of condemnation had glory, the ministry of righteousness exceeds much more in glory.
NKJV.10: For even what was made glorious had no glory in this respect, because of the glory that excels.
NKJV.11: For if what is passing away was glorious, what remains is much more glorious.
NKJV.12: Therefore, since we have such hope, we use great boldness of speech—
NKJV.13: unlike Moses, who put a veil over his face so that the children of Israel could not look steadily at the end of what was passing away.
NKJV.14: But their minds were blinded. For until this day the same veil remains unlifted in the reading of the Old Testament, because the veil is taken away in Christ.
NKJV.15: But even to this day, when Moses is read, a veil lies on their heart.
NKJV.16: Nevertheless when one turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
NKJV.17: Now the Lord is the Spirit; and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.
NKJV.18: But we all, with unveiled face, beholding as in a mirror the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into the same image from glory to glory, just as by the Spirit of the Lord.

ESV.1: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? Or do we need, as some do, letters of recommendation to you, or from you?
ESV.2: You yourselves are our letter of recommendation, written on our  hearts, to be known and read by all.
ESV.3: And you show that you are a letter from Christ delivered by us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
ESV.4: Such is the confidence that we have through Christ toward God.
ESV.5: Not that we are sufficient in ourselves to claim anything as coming from us, but our sufficiency is from God,
ESV.6: who has made us sufficient to be ministers of a new covenant, not of the letter but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
ESV.7: Now if the ministry of death, carved in letters on stone, came with such glory that the Israelites could not gaze at Moses' face because of its glory, which was being brought to an end,
ESV.8: will not the ministry of the Spirit have even more glory?
ESV.9: For if there was glory in the ministry of condemnation, the ministry of righteousness must far exceed it in glory.
ESV.10: Indeed, in this case, what once had glory has come to have no glory at all, because of the glory that surpasses it.
ESV.11: For if what was being brought to an end came with glory, much more will what is permanent have glory.
ESV.12: Since we have such a hope, we are very bold,
ESV.13: not like Moses, who would put a veil over his face so that the Israelites might not gaze at the outcome of what was being brought to an end.
ESV.14: But their minds were hardened. For to this day, when they read the old covenant, that same veil remains unlifted, because only through Christ is it taken away.
ESV.15: Yes, to this day whenever Moses is read a veil lies over their hearts.
ESV.16: But when one  turns to the Lord, the veil is removed.
ESV.17: Now the Lord  is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
ESV.18: And we all, with unveiled face, beholding the glory of the Lord,  are being transformed into the same image from one degree of glory to another.  For this comes from the Lord who is the Spirit.

NASB.1: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? Or do we need, as some, letters of commendation to you or from you?
NASB.2: You are our letter, written in our hearts, known and read by all men;
NASB.3: being manifested that you are a letter of Christ, cared for by us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
NASB.4: Such confidence we have through Christ toward God.
NASB.5: Not that we are adequate in ourselves to consider anything as [coming] from ourselves, but our adequacy is from God,
NASB.6: who also made us adequate [as] servants of a new covenant, not of the letter but of the Spirit; for the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
NASB.7: But if the ministry of death, in letters engraved on stones, came with glory, so that the sons of Israel could not look intently at the face of Moses because of the glory of his face, fading [as] it was,
NASB.8: how will the ministry of the Spirit fail to be even more with glory?
NASB.9: For if the ministry of condemnation has glory, much more does the ministry of righteousness abound in glory.
NASB.10: For indeed what had glory, in this case has no glory because of the glory that surpasses [it].
NASB.11: For if that which fades away [was] with glory, much more that which remains [is] in glory.
NASB.12: Therefore having such a hope, we use great boldness in [our] speech,
NASB.13: and [are] not like Moses, [who] used to put a veil over his face so that the sons of Israel would not look intently at the end of what was fading away.
NASB.14: But their minds were hardened; for until this very day at the reading of the old covenant the same veil remains unlifted, because it is removed in Christ.
NASB.15: But to this day whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their heart;
NASB.16: but whenever a person turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
NASB.17: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, [there] is liberty.
NASB.18: But we all, with unveiled face, beholding as in a mirror the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into the same image from glory to glory, just as from the Lord, the Spirit.

NLT.1: Are we beginning to praise ourselves again? Are we like others, who need to bring you letters of recommendation, or who ask you to write such letters on their behalf? Surely not!
NLT.2: The only letter of recommendation we need is you yourselves. Your lives are a letter written in our hearts; everyone can read it and recognize our good work among you.
NLT.3: Clearly, you are a letter from Christ showing the result of our ministry among you. This “letter” is written not with pen and ink, but with the Spirit of the living God. It is carved not on tablets of stone, but on human hearts.
NLT.4: We are confident of all this because of our great trust in God through Christ.
NLT.5: It is not that we think we are qualified to do anything on our own. Our qualification comes from God.
NLT.6: He has enabled us to be ministers of his new covenant. This is a covenant not of written laws, but of the Spirit. The old written covenant ends in death; but under the new covenant, the Spirit gives life.
NLT.7: The old way, with laws etched in stone, led to death, though it began with such glory that the people of Israel could not bear to look at Moses’ face. For his face shone with the glory of God, even though the brightness was already fading away.
NLT.8: Shouldn’t we expect far greater glory under the new way, now that the Holy Spirit is giving life?
NLT.9: If the old way, which brings condemnation, was glorious, how much more glorious is the new way, which makes us right with God!
NLT.10: In fact, that first glory was not glorious at all compared with the overwhelming glory of the new way.
NLT.11: So if the old way, which has been replaced, was glorious, how much more glorious is the new, which remains forever!
NLT.12: Since this new way gives us such confidence, we can be very bold.
NLT.13: We are not like Moses, who put a veil over his face so the people of Israel would not see the glory, even though it was destined to fade away.
NLT.14: But the people’s minds were hardened, and to this day whenever the old covenant is being read, the same veil covers their minds so they cannot understand the truth. And this veil can be removed only by believing in Christ.
NLT.15: Yes, even today when they read Moses’ writings, their hearts are covered with that veil, and they do not understand.
NLT.16: But whenever someone turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
NLT.17: For the Lord is the Spirit, and wherever the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
NLT.18: So all of us who have had that veil removed can see and reflect the glory of the Lord. And the Lord — who is the Spirit — makes us more and more like him as we are changed into his glorious image.

WEB.1: Are we beginning again to commend ourselves? Or do we need, as do some, letters of commendation to you or from you?
WEB.2: You are our letter, written in our hearts, known and read by all men;
WEB.3: being revealed that you are a letter of Christ, served by us, written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in tablets of stone, but in tablets that are hearts of flesh.
WEB.4: Such confidence we have through Christ toward God;
WEB.5: not that we are sufficient of ourselves, to account anything as from ourselves; but our sufficiency is from God;
WEB.6: who also made us sufficient as servants of a new covenant; not of the letter, but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
WEB.7: But if the service of death, written engraved on stones, came with glory, so that the children of Israel could not look steadfastly on the face of Moses for the glory of his face; which was passing away:
WEB.8: won’t service of the Spirit be with much more glory?
WEB.9: For if the service of condemnation has glory, the service of righteousness exceeds much more in glory.
WEB.10: For most certainly that which has been made glorious has not been made glorious in this respect, by reason of the glory that surpasses.
WEB.11: For if that which passes away was with glory, much more that which remains is in glory.
WEB.12: Having therefore such a hope, we use great boldness of speech,
WEB.13: and not as Moses, who put a veil on his face, that the children of Israel wouldn’t look steadfastly on the end of that which was passing away.
WEB.14: But their minds were hardened, for until this very day at the reading of the old covenant the same veil remains, because in Christ it passes away.
WEB.15: But to this day, when Moses is read, a veil lies on their heart.
WEB.16: But whenever one turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
WEB.17: Now the Lord is the Spirit and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.
WEB.18: But we all, with unveiled face seeing the glory of the Lord as in a mirror, are transformed into the same image from glory to glory, even as from the Lord, the Spirit.

CSB17.1: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? Or do we need, like some, letters of recommendation to you or from you?
CSB17.2: You yourselves are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.3: You show that you are Christ’s letter, <sup>ⓑ</sup> delivered <sup>[1]</sup> by us, not written with ink but with the Spirit of the living God <sup>ⓒ</sup>  — not on tablets of stone <sup>ⓓ</sup> but on tablets of human hearts. <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.4: Such is the confidence we have through Christ before God.
CSB17.5: It is not that we are competent in <sup>[3]</sup> ourselves to claim anything as coming from ourselves, but our adequacy is from God. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.6: He has made us competent to be ministers of a new covenant, <sup>ⓖ</sup> not of the letter, <sup>ⓗ</sup> but of the Spirit. For the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
CSB17.7: Now if the ministry that brought death, chiseled in letters on stones, came with glory, <sup>ⓘ</sup> so that the Israelites were not able to gaze steadily at Moses’s face because of its glory, which was set aside,
CSB17.8: how will the ministry of the Spirit not be more glorious?
CSB17.9: For if the ministry that brought condemnation had glory, the ministry that brings righteousness overflows with even more glory.
CSB17.10: In fact, what had been glorious is not glorious now by comparison because of the glory that surpasses it.
CSB17.11: For if what was set aside <sup>ⓙ</sup> was glorious, what endures will be even more glorious.
CSB17.12: Since, then, we have such a hope, <sup>ⓚ</sup> we act with great boldness.
CSB17.13: We are not like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face <sup>ⓛ</sup> to prevent the Israelites from gazing steadily until the end <sup>[4]</sup> of the glory of what was being set aside,
CSB17.14: but their minds were hardened. <sup>ⓜ</sup> For to this day, at the reading of the old covenant, <sup>ⓝ</sup> the same veil remains; it is not lifted, because it is set aside only in Christ. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.15: Yet still today, whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their hearts,
CSB17.16: but whenever a person turns <sup>ⓑ</sup> to the Lord, the veil is removed. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.17: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
CSB17.18: We all, with unveiled faces, are looking as in a mirror at <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup> the glory of the Lord <sup>ⓔ</sup> and are being transformed <sup>ⓕ</sup> into the same image <sup>ⓖ</sup> from glory to glory; this is from the Lord who is the Spirit. <sup>[6]</sup>

AMP.1: Are we starting to commend ourselves again? Or do we need, like some [false teachers], letters of recommendation to you or from you? [No!]
AMP.2: You are our letter [of recommendation], written in our hearts, recognized and read by everyone.
AMP.3: You show that you are a letter from Christ, delivered by us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
AMP.4: Such is the confidence and steadfast reliance and absolute trust that we have through Christ toward God.
AMP.5: Not that we are sufficiently qualified in ourselves to claim anything as coming from us, but our sufficiency and qualifications come from God.
AMP.6: He has qualified us [making us sufficient] as ministers of a new covenant [of salvation through Christ], not of the letter [of a written code] but of the Spirit; for the letter [of the Law] kills [by revealing sin and demanding obedience], but the Spirit gives life.
AMP.7: Now if the ministry of death, engraved in letters on stones [the covenant of the Law which led to death because of sin], came with such glory and splendor that the Israelites were not able to look steadily at the face of Moses because of its glory, [a brilliance] that was fading,
AMP.8: how will the ministry of the Spirit [the new covenant which allows us to be Spirit-filled] fail to be even more glorious and splendid?
AMP.9: For if the ministry that brings condemnation [the old covenant, the Law] has glory, how much more does glory overflow in the ministry that brings righteousness [the new covenant which declares believers free of guilt and sets them apart for God's special purpose]!
AMP.10: Indeed, what had glory [the Law], in this case no longer has glory because of the glory that surpasses it [the gospel].
AMP.11: For if that [Law] which fades away came with glory, how much more must that [gospel] which remains and is permanent abide in glory and splendor!
AMP.12: Since we have such a [glorious] hope and confident expectation, we speak with great courage,
AMP.13: and we are not like Moses, who used to put a veil over his face so that the Israelites would not gaze at the end of the glory which was fading away.
AMP.14: But [in fact] their minds were hardened [for they had lost the ability to understand]; for until this very day at the reading of the old covenant the same veil remains unlifted, because it is removed [only] in Christ.
AMP.15: But to this day whenever Moses is read, a veil [of blindness] lies over their heart;
AMP.16: but whenever a person turns [in repentance and faith] to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
AMP.17: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty [emancipation from bondage, true freedom].
AMP.18: And we all, with unveiled face, continually seeing as in a mirror the glory of the Lord, are progressively being transformed into His image from [one degree of] glory to [even more] glory, which comes from the Lord, [who is] the Spirit.

MSG.1: Does it sound like we're patting ourselves on the back, insisting on our credentials, asserting our authority? Well, we're not. Neither do we need letters of endorsement, either to you or from you.
MSG.2: You yourselves are all the endorsement we need. Your very lives are a letter that anyone can read by just looking at you.
MSG.3: Christ himself wrote it—not with ink, but with God's living Spirit; not chiseled into stone, but carved into human lives—and we publish it.
MSG.4: We couldn't be more sure of ourselves in this—that you, written by Christ himself for God, are our letter of recommendation.
MSG.5: We wouldn't think of writing this kind of letter about ourselves. Only God can write such a letter.
MSG.6: His letter authorizes us to help carry out this new plan of action. The plan wasn't written out with ink on paper, with pages and pages of legal footnotes, killing your spirit. It's written with Spirit on spirit, his life on our lives!
MSG.7: The Government of Death, its constitution chiseled on stone tablets, had a dazzling inaugural. Moses' face as he delivered the tablets was so bright that day (even though it would fade soon enough) that the people of Israel could no more look right at him than stare into the sun.
MSG.8: How much more dazzling, then, the Government of Living Spirit?
MSG.9: If the Government of Condemnation was impressive, how about this Government of Affirmation?
MSG.10: Bright as that old government was, it would look downright dull alongside this new one.
MSG.11: If that makeshift arrangement impressed us, how much more this brightly shining government installed for eternity?
MSG.12: With that kind of hope to excite us, nothing holds us back.
MSG.13: Unlike Moses, we have nothing to hide. Everything is out in the open with us. He wore a veil so the children of Israel wouldn't notice that the glory was fading away—
MSG.14: and they didn't notice. They didn't notice it then and they don't notice it now, don't notice that there's nothing left behind that veil.
MSG.15: Even today when the proclamations of that old, bankrupt government are read out, they can't see through it. Only Christ can get rid of the veil so they can see for themselves that there's nothing there.
MSG.16: Whenever, though, they turn to face God as Moses did, God removes the veil and there they are—face to face!
MSG.17: They suddenly recognize that God is a living, personal presence, not a piece of chiseled stone. And when God is personally present, a living Spirit, that old, constricting legislation is recognized as obsolete. We're free of it!
MSG.18: All of us! Nothing between us and God, our faces shining with the brightness of his face. And so we are transfigured much like the Messiah, our lives gradually becoming brighter and more beautiful as God enters our lives and we become like him.

NIV.1: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? Or do we need, like some people, letters of recommendation to you or from you?
NIV.2: You yourselves are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everybody.
NIV.3: You show that you are a letter from Christ, the result of our ministry, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
NIV.4: Such confidence as this is ours through Christ before God.
NIV.5: Not that we are competent in ourselves to claim anything for ourselves, but our competence comes from God.
NIV.6: He has made us competent as ministers of a new covenant—not of the letter but of the Spirit; for the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
NIV.7:
The Glory of the New Covenant
Now if the ministry that brought death, which was engraved in letters on stone, came with glory, so that the Israelites could not look steadily at the face of Moses because of its glory, fading though it was,
NIV.8: will not the ministry of the Spirit be even more glorious?
NIV.9: If the ministry that condemns men is glorious, how much more glorious is the ministry that brings righteousness!
NIV.10: For what was glorious has no glory now in comparison with the surpassing glory.
NIV.11: And if what was fading away came with glory, how much greater is the glory of that which lasts!
NIV.12: Therefore, since we have such a hope, we are very bold.
NIV.13: We are not like Moses, who would put a veil over his face to keep the Israelites from gazing at it while the radiance was fading away.
NIV.14: But their minds were made dull, for to this day the same veil remains when the old covenant is read. It has not been removed, because only in Christ is it taken away.
NIV.15: Even to this day when Moses is read, a veil covers their hearts.
NIV.16: But whenever anyone turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away.
NIV.17: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
NIV.18: And we, who with unveiled faces all reflect the Lord’s glory, are being transformed into his likeness with ever-increasing glory, which comes from the Lord, who is the Spirit.

NRSVCE.1: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? Surely we do not need, as some do, letters of recommendation to you or from you, do we?
NRSVCE.2: You yourselves are our letter, written on our hearts, to be known and read by all;
NRSVCE.3: and you show that you are a letter of Christ, prepared by us, written not with ink but with the Spirit of the living God, not on tablets of stone but on tablets of human hearts.
NRSVCE.4: Such is the confidence that we have through Christ toward God.
NRSVCE.5: Not that we are competent of ourselves to claim anything as coming from us; our competence is from God,
NRSVCE.6: who has made us competent to be ministers of a new covenant, not of letter but of spirit; for the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
NRSVCE.7: Now if the ministry of death, chiseled in letters on stone tablets, came in glory so that the people of Israel could not gaze at Moses’ face because of the glory of his face, a glory now set aside,
NRSVCE.8: how much more will the ministry of the Spirit come in glory?
NRSVCE.9: For if there was glory in the ministry of condemnation, much more does the ministry of justification abound in glory!
NRSVCE.10: Indeed, what once had glory has lost its glory because of the greater glory;
NRSVCE.11: for if what was set aside came through glory, much more has the permanent come in glory!
NRSVCE.12: Since, then, we have such a hope, we act with great boldness,
NRSVCE.13: not like Moses, who put a veil over his face to keep the people of Israel from gazing at the end of the glory that was being set aside.
NRSVCE.14: But their minds were hardened. Indeed, to this very day, when they hear the reading of the old covenant, that same veil is still there, since only in Christ is it set aside.
NRSVCE.15: Indeed, to this very day whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their minds;
NRSVCE.16: but when one turns to the Lord, the veil is removed.
NRSVCE.17: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is freedom.
NRSVCE.18: And all of us, with unveiled faces, seeing the glory of the Lord as though reflected in a mirror, are being transformed into the same image from one degree of glory to another; for this comes from the Lord, the Spirit.

NET.1: Are we beginning to commend ourselves again? We don’t need letters of recommendation to you or from you as some other people do, do we?
NET.2: You yourselves are our letter, written on our hearts, known and read by everyone,
NET.3: revealing that you are a letter of Christ, delivered by us, written not with ink but by the Spirit of the living God, not on stone tablets but on tablets of human hearts.
NET.4: Now we have such confidence in God through Christ.
NET.5: Not that we are adequate in ourselves to consider anything as if it were coming from ourselves, but our adequacy is from God,
NET.6: who made us adequate to be servants of a new covenant not based on the letter but on the Spirit, for the letter kills, but the Spirit gives life.
NET.7: But if the ministry that produced death — carved in letters on stone tablets — came with glory, so that the Israelites could not keep their eyes fixed on the face of Moses because of the glory of his face (a glory which was made ineffective),
NET.8: how much more glorious will the ministry of the Spirit be?
NET.9: For if there was glory in the ministry that produced condemnation, how much more does the ministry that produces righteousness excel in glory!
NET.10: For indeed, what had been glorious now has no glory because of the tremendously greater glory of what replaced it.
NET.11: For if what was made ineffective came with glory, how much more has what remains come in glory!
NET.12: Therefore, since we have such a hope, we behave with great boldness,
NET.13: and not like Moses who used to put a veil over his face to keep the Israelites from staring at the result of the glory that was made ineffective.
NET.14: But their minds were closed. For to this very day, the same veil remains when they hear the old covenant read. It has not been removed because only in Christ is it taken away.
NET.15: But until this very day whenever Moses is read, a veil lies over their minds,
NET.16: but when one turns to the Lord, the veil is removed.
NET.17: Now the Lord is the Spirit, and where the Spirit of the Lord is present, there is freedom.
NET.18: And we all, with unveiled faces reflecting the glory of the Lord, are being transformed into the same image from one degree of glory to another, which is from the Lord, who is the Spirit.
